name: Firstrade
description_short: Commission-free US broker with $0 stock, ETF, and options trades and no per-contract fees — a standout for cost-conscious options traders.
description_long:
Firstrade Securities is a US-based online brokerage founded in 1985, making it one of the longest-running discount brokers in the country. Originally serving the Chinese-American community in New York, it has grown into a fully licensed broker-dealer offering commission-free trading on stocks, ETFs, options, and mutual funds to a broad retail audience across the United States.

What sets Firstrade apart is its options pricing model: $0 per trade with no per-contract fee. Most competitors charge $0.65 per contract, so active options traders can save significantly, especially on multi-leg strategies or high-volume trades. This pricing structure makes Firstrade one of the most cost-effective platforms for retail options traders in the US market.

The platform supports a wide range of account types including individual taxable accounts, joint accounts, IRAs (Traditional, Roth, SEP, SIMPLE), custodial accounts, and investment club accounts. Firstrade also offers a well-rated mobile app, real-time quotes, price alerts, research tools, and educational resources to support traders at various experience levels.

The trade-off is that Firstrade lacks some of the advanced tools found on platforms like Thinkorswim or Interactive Brokers — no paper trading simulator, no proprietary screener with deep customization, and no API access for algorithmic strategies. It is best suited for buy-and-hold investors, options traders who prioritize zero per-contract fees, and bilingual (English/Chinese) users who value multilingual customer support.
affiliate_commission: Cash bonus per referred account (varies by promotion)
pros: $0 options trades with no per-contract fee — rare among US brokers
Truly commission-free on stocks, ETFs, and mutual funds
Wide range of account types including multiple IRA varieties
Bilingual support in English and Chinese
Long-established broker with 35+ years of operating history
cons: No paper trading or simulated account for practice
Platform and charting tools are basic compared to TD Ameritrade or IBKR
No API access for algorithmic or automated trading
best_for: options traders seeking zero per-contract fees
